probe: C33 (h) — selfterm-disabled cold-boot of Wan 1.3B t2v on RunPod + ComfyUI
scope: Isolate whether the in-pod selfterm watchdog is the source of the T+15s bash-exit/rc=0/restart-loop mystery seen in Q4. NULL result (cycle persists) rules selfterm out and points root cause at image entrypoint, docker daemon, or RunPod container manager.
gpu: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 (community-cloud) @ $0.22/hr (RTX 4090 first-choice was out of capacity; fell through to A5000/3090 per cfg gpu_preference)
cfg: tests/live/cfg_c28_phase_a_diagnostic.yaml (idle_timeout=25m, max_lifetime=90m, job_timeout=15m, time_buffer=5m → selfterm dead-man window = 50min)

intervention.mechanism: RunPodProvider.create_instance() injects KINOFORGE_SELFTERM_SCRIPT="" when KINOFORGE_DIAG_DISABLE_SELFTERM=1 is set in the operator shell env. The in-pod bash guard `if [ -n "${KINOFORGE_SELFTERM_SCRIPT:-}" ]; then ... fi` (asserted by tests/engines/test_comfyui_render_provision.py::test_render_provision_selfterm_launch_is_optional_when_env_unset) short-circuits, so `nohup python3 /tmp/selfterm.py` is never executed.
intervention.command: KINOFORGE_DIAG_DISABLE_SELFTERM=1 timeout 900 pixi run kinoforge generate -c tests/live/cfg_c28_phase_a_diagnostic.yaml --prompt examples/configs/prompts/dawn-flight.md --mode t2v --no-reuse

exit_trap_uploads.last: 2026-06-17T00:26:40+00:00 (and beyond — final 27th upload at run-end window)

q4_baseline.interpretation: Q4 ran the same cfg + prompt with selfterm ENABLED. 29 EXIT-trap uploads in ~15 min wall, ~31s cycle. Same image, same provision script, same GPU class.

delta_vs_q4.uploads: 27 vs 29 (−2, within capacity-retry noise: 4090 capacity-fallthrough ate ~10s at probe start before A5000/3090 cold-booted)

sample_exit_trap_payload.interpretation: Identical to Q4's failure-mode fingerprint: bash exits with rc=0 partway through provision (silent pip install -q phase, tee buffer's last visible line is the prior `Cloning into 'ComfyUI'...`), GPU 0%, no real workload yet. Container restart loop driven by RunPod's restart=always docker policy (Q9 verified).

verdict: H_h REFUTED — selfterm is NOT the cause of the T+15s bash-exit. With selfterm fully disabled (`nohup python3 /tmp/selfterm.py` never launches; KINOFORGE_SELFTERM_SCRIPT env var empty), the Wan 1.3B t2v cold-boot still exhibits a ~32s restart-loop cycle with rc=0 mid-provision — statistically identical to Q4's selfterm-enabled baseline.
root_cause_funnel: (2) RULED OUT — script truncation (Q7: /tmp/p.sh intact at 86 lines / 5373 bytes).
(3) RULED OUT — tee buffering (Q8: stdbuf -oL changed nothing).
(4) RULED OUT — documented selfterm timer conditions (Q5/Q6: dead-man 50min, max-lifetime 85min, neither hits at T+15s).

(5) REMAINING CANDIDATES — image entrypoint (runpod/pytorch:2.4.0-py3.11-cuda12.4.1-devel-ubuntu22.04 may run its own SIGTERM under conditions we haven't characterised), docker daemon (OOM-killer, memory ceiling, exec resource limit), RunPod container manager (pod-side `dockerArgs` wrapper, side-process supervisor).
next_steps_unblocked: (h) NEXT — investigate image entrypoint: spin a $0.005 probe with `bash -c 'echo START; sleep 30; echo STILL_HERE; sleep 90; echo END'` as dockerArgs (no kinoforge provision script, no selfterm, no aws/git/pip) on the same image. If THIS bash exits at T+15s, the image entrypoint is killing it. If it survives 120s, the kill is triggered by something the provision script does in its first 15s (apt? pip? wget?).
(i) STILL BLOCKED — C25 production heartbeat enable. Until (h)-continuation isolates the kill source, Wan workloads cannot cold-boot regardless of whether `podEditJob` restarts containers.
cleanup_followup: Revert e66ec99 after evidence committed (Q5-Q8 throwaway pattern).
